rulings.Case Title: DONALD NORMAN VS. PATRICK STRATEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Settlement And Reinstate Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6 And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities [Redacted Version]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 3. 1-DEFENDANTS PATRICK STRATEMAN, and JAMIE STRATEMAN'S Motion To Approve Settlement And Reinstate Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6. [Redacted Version] Defendants Patrick Strateman and Jamie Strateman's motion to approve the settlement entered among the parties to this case mid-trial is granted. The court finds that the settlement reached by the parties here of derivative claims is fair, reasonable, and not the product of fraud, overreaching, or collusion by the settling parties. The judgment entered by the court on April 8, 2024, is reinstated. The court grants the parties' requests for judicial notice and addresses objections to evidence only where relevant to the reasoning of this order. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Legal Recover Llc To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 4. PLAINTIFF BRYANT FU'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Defendant Legal Recover Llc To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions. The court construes plaintiff Bryant Fu's motion as a motion to compel defendant Legal Recover LLC to respond to his request for production of documents, and for sanctions. The motion is granted. Fu provides a declaration that he served the RFPs on Legal Recover by email. His declaration attaches a copy of the delivery email. The email address to which Fu addressed the RFPs is also listed on Legal Recover's counsel's caption. (See Fu Decl., Ex. 2 & para. 2.) The court concludes that the RFPs were served. Because Legal Recover did not timely respond, it has waived objections. Legal Recover is ordered to respond to the RFPs with complete, verified, objection-free responses within two weeks of this order. Legal Recover shall produce all responsive documents within 30 days of this order. Fu also seeks sanctions; he asks for $560 in fees and costs. (Fu Decl., para. 9.) The court concludes that Legal Recover's discovery position lacks substantial justification and orders Legal Recover to pay $560 to Plaintiff within 30 days of this order. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c).) Plaintiff provides a declaration that he incurred fees in connection with the motion. The court does not award separate sanctions payable to the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses, Without Objections, To Requests For Production (Set One) To Plaintiffs Patricia Duarte, Luis Gutierrez, Thomas Duarte, Nicole Gutierrez, And Genesis Gutierrez; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,566.00 Against Plaintiffs And/Or Their Attorneys Of Record; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Lori Cataldo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 6. DEFENDANT SABINA YUEN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Responses, Without Objections, To Requests For Production (Set One) To Plaintiffs Patricia Duarte, Luis Gutierrez, Thomas Duarte, Nicole Gutierrez, And Genesis Gutierrez; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,566.00 Against Plaintiffs And/Or Their Attorneys Of Record. The motion to compel responses to requests for production is denied as moot. Plaintiffs provide proof that they electronically served the RFP responses by email on March 16, 2026, before Defendant filed this motion on April 6, 2026. Defendant shall pay $500 as a sanction to Plaintiff Patricia Duarte within 30 days of this order; moving for relief without disclosing to the court that responses had already been served (or, if the responses were overlooked before the motion was filed, failing to take the motion off calendar upon discovering the oversight) lacks substantial justification. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum And Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 8. PLAINTIFFS ANDRE WILLIAMS, and TIMOJENE WATSON'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum And Protective Order. Hearing required. Plaintiffs Andre Williams and Timojene Watson move for a protective order to prevent defendant the San Francisco Housing Authority from obtaining Williams's medical records. The Housing Authority apparently seeks records from Dr. Daniel J. Buckley. Plaintiffs did not provide a copy of the subpoena in their filing. The basis for the request, according to the Housing Authority's opposition, is that Williams has repeatedly delayed his deposition based on medical reasons, and provided a partial and redacted record of Buckley's treatment as justification. The court is inclined to deny the protective order and permit documents to be subpoenaed. By citing Buckley's records, Williams has put them at issue. "When a party raises [his] physical condition as an issue in a case, [he] waives the right to claim that the relevant medical records are privileged." (Vesco v. Superior Court (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 275, 279.) Vesco holds that the waiver occurs even where a party simply seeks accommodations in the litigation process. However, a simpler solution is apparent: Williams can submit to deposition. The court is aware that it previously denied Williams's request for a protective order to preclude a deposition, yet the deposition has not yet occurred. The court is inclined to continue this motion for hearing for two weeks to allow Williams to attend his deposition in the interim. If the deposition has been completed by the continued hearing date, the subpoena will be mooted and the protective order can be granted. If the deposition has not been completed, the court will issue the above ruling as the order of the court and deny the protective order as to the subpoena. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 10. DEFENDANT ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC's MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. The court lifts its stay of its sanctions order in part. Plaintiff Alexander Xue shall pay $1500 to Round One within 30 days; the remaining $500 sanction is permanently stayed. The court previously issued partial sanctions of $2000 against plaintiff Alexander Xue for failure to comply with the court's October 31, 2025, order to (1) amend his privilege log; (2) make further responses to RFP nos. 1-11, 13-14, and 17; and (3) pay sanctions of $750 to Round One. The court set a deadline of February 9, 2026 for Xue to amend his privilege log and responses, and stayed its sanctions order through March 20, 2026, to incentivize Xue to comply fully. The court also ordered the parties to meet and confer about the February 9, 2026 amendments. With the parties' agreement, the court also offered to conduct an informal IDC and review lodged work product over which there was continued dispute. Later, the parties stipulated to continue the hearing on this motion to April 1, 2026. Round One submits a declaration and brief that Xue amended his privilege log on February 9 but has not amended his RFP responses and has not responded to meet and confer efforts. Xue has not submitted a brief. The court permanently stays $500 of the sanction in light of the amended log but the stay as to the remaining $1500 is lifted; this sanction shall be paid by Xue to Round One within 30 days. Xue is ordered to make further responses to RFP nos. 1-11, 13-14, and 17 and to pay the outstanding $750 sanctions from the October 31 order forthwith. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA BUNTING VS. THE REALREAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (RE-SET PER THE COURT'S MAR-24-2026 RULING)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar APR-29-2026. LINE 15. DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is GRANTED. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
